refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency and its compatibility with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. The FR does not contain multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria defined in the worksite safety plan and operational policies, specifically that the p95 latency is ≤ 150 ms.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms to maintain latency within threshold
Separate teleoperation control commands from non-critical data streams to reduce communication load
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules could increase coupling
Shared data buffers or synchronous processing might create dependencies
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a DP focused on system architecture and data flow allows modular separation of communication and control, supporting independence and easier latency management.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing delays
Predictive control algorithms may be needed if latency approaches threshold, adding complexity
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency monitoring and control mechanisms as a complementary DP to enhance robustness against variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering strategies across the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data streams for processing and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange with external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is designed to handle core data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module handles physical operations; real-time data processing is relevant but not central, leading to moderate diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control but less focused on core data processing architecture, causing higher diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, less on data processing, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing a system architecture prioritizing real-time data processing and minimal buffering, as it centralizes data handling and processing logic.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some real-time processing is embedded in vehicle hardware.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services may require careful coordination to avoid unintended diffusion of processing logic.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the required teleoperation latency for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some real-time processing is embedded in vehicle hardware.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services may require careful coordination to avoid unintended diffusion of processing logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating it is an appropriate and cohesive boundary for the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, suggesting some risk if processing logic spreads outside NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring end-to-end teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions, which can be traced to the NGHE Solution module's performance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which encompasses the core architecture for real-time data processing and buffering, aligning well with the FR's requirements.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to minimize diffusion risk.
Investigate and explicitly define any real-time processing responsibilities within the Vehicle module to avoid overlap.
Establish coordination protocols to ensure processing logic remains centralized within NGHE Solution where possible, reducing architectural diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP logically addresses the FR, and the IM allocation is clear and cohesive with low diffusion risk. Although some diffusion risks exist, they are manageable through interface clarifications and coordination protocols without requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and contained module boundary is preferable to avoid complexity and potential diffusion of processing logic.

revise.why: Clarify and document interface contracts and coordination protocols between NGHE Solution and related modules to minimize diffusion risk and avoid overlap in real-time processing responsibilities.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively centralize real-time processing logic.
Interface clarifications and coordination protocols will sufficiently mitigate diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap of real-time processing in Vehicle module.
Unintended diffusion of processing logic across Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Verification results showing end-to-end teleoperation latency meeting requirements under network conditions.
Assessment of real-time processing responsibilities within Vehicle module.
